_description: Iconographic family assignments for the active rongorongo corpus sign inventory. Based on Barthel (1958) morphological taxonomy as documented in Fischer (1997) and Horley (2021). Eight families: anthropomorphic (human features), zoomorphic (animals/birds), botanical (plants/vegetation), celestial (astronomical symbols), geometric (abstract shapes), composite (two or more combined elements), positional (orientation variants of a base sign), unknown (genuinely uncertain). Signs are assigned to 'unknown' when the iconographic affiliation cannot be determined with confidence from the published literature.
_source_references: Barthel, T.S. (1958). Grundlagen zur Entzifferung der Osterinselschrift. Hamburg: Cram, de Gruyter.
Fischer, S.R. (1997). Rongorongo: The Easter Island Script. Oxford: Clarendon Press.
Horley, P. (2021). Rongorongo Sign Inventory. Rapa Nui Journal 35(1).
_notes: Signs 200-291: anthropomorphic head series (variant head types). Signs 300-390: anthropomorphic body series. Signs 600-699: bird-headed/zoomorphic series. Signs 700-799: animal/fish zoomorphic series. Signs 400-591: composite/combined-element series. Signs 001-099: iconographically mixed; geometric signs are basic abstract shapes; botanical signs are plant/fern/vegetation forms (primarily 040-099 per Fischer 1997); celestial signs are astronomical motifs; unknown for genuinely ambiguous forms.
